item.title: US regulators missed the July 18 GENIUS Act deadline; no final stablecoin rules published as the comment period extends to August 21.
MiCA's transition period ended July 1; EU crypto-asset service providers without CASP authorization now face penalties up to 15 million euros.
SEC added three Regulation Crypto rulemakings to its July 2026 agenda; final rules are not expected before mid-2027.
US and UK treasuries issued a 10-point digital asset roadmap July 14, targeting tokenized securities and cross-border stablecoin activity.
UK Treasury formed a 54-firm tokenization taskforce on July 13 including BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, HSBC, Fidelity, and DTCC.
BNY Mellon and State Street launched digital-asset custody platforms following the rescission of SAB 121.
BlackRock and JPMorgan filed tokenized Treasury funds as eligible stablecoin reserve assets under the GENIUS Act framework.
BlackRock is readying the launch of two tokenized money-market funds as demand for on-chain Treasury products grows.
Citadel Securities led a $400 million investment in Crypto.com at a $20 billion valuation on July 16.
Spot Ethereum ETFs drew $9.3 billion in net July inflows, outpacing Bitcoin ETF flows as demand for Ethereum-based settlement increased.
US Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs attracted $281.8 million in combined inflows, ending an eight-week, $8.26 billion outflow streak.
On-chain real-world asset tokenization surpassed $20 billion, with BlackRock's BUIDL fund alone exceeding $2.5 billion.
Crypto losses totaled $1.315 billion across 344 incidents in H1 2026, with 44% stemming from two infrastructure security breaches.
DeFi protocol Ostium paused trading after an $18 million exploit and is investigating the breach.
The OCC published a GENIUS Act implementing proposal with a $5 million minimum capital floor for new stablecoin issuers.
